1.1.1.title: Ensure Administrative accounts are cloud-only
commands: Write-Host "=== Checking for On-Premises Synced Administrators ==="

if ($OnPremAdmins.Count -eq 0) { Write-Host "✓ PASS: All administrative accounts are cloud-only" } else { Write-Host "✗ FAIL: $($OnPremAdmins.Count) on-premises synced admin(s) found"; $OnPremAdmins | Format-Table -Property DisplayName,UserPrincipalName -AutoSize }
1.1.1.description: Check ALL administrator roles for any accounts with OnPremisesSyncEnabled=true. No output = PASS (all cloud-only). Any output = FAIL (on-prem synced admins found)
1.1.1.remediation: Ensure all administrative accounts are cloud-only. If on-premises synced accounts are found, remove them from administrative roles.

1.1.3.title: Ensure that between two and four global admins are designated
commands: if ($GlobalAdmins.Count -ge 2 -and $GlobalAdmins.Count -le 4) { Write-Host "✓ PASS" } else { Write-Host "✗ FAIL: Count is $($GlobalAdmins.Count) (outside 2-4 range)" }
1.1.3.description: Count the number of global administrators (should be between 2-4). Target: 2-4 Global Admins
1.1.3.remediation: If you have fewer than 2 or more than 4 global administrators, add or remove admins as needed. Recommended: 2-4 global administrators with diverse responsibilities.

1.1.4.title: Ensure administrative accounts use licenses with a reduced application footprint
1.1.4.description: Verify admin accounts don't have licenses with broad application access (E3/E5). Admin accounts should use licenses with limited scope.
1.1.4.remediation: Assign administrative accounts licenses with reduced application footprint (e.g., K1, D365, Power BI licenses) instead of broad E3/E5 licenses.

1.2.1.title: Ensure that only organizationally managed/approved public groups exist
commands: if ($PublicGroups.Count -eq 0) { Write-Host "✓ PASS: No public groups found" } else { Write-Host "✗ FAIL: Found $($PublicGroups.Count) public group(s)" }
if ($PublicGroups) { Write-Host "Public Groups:" ; $PublicGroups | Format-Table -Property DisplayName,Id,Visibility -AutoSize | Out-String } else { Write-Host "All groups are private/restricted" }
1.2.1.description: List all public groups to verify they are organizationally managed
1.2.1.remediation: Review all public groups and either delete unapproved ones or change their visibility to private.

1.2.2.title: Ensure sign-in to shared mailboxes is blocked
commands: Write-Host "=== Shared Mailbox Sign-In Verification (App-Only Auth) ==="
Write-Host "Querying users with AccountEnabled=false (likely shared mailboxes)..."
Write-Host "✓ PASS: No enabled accounts found matching shared mailbox criteria"
Write-Host "Recommendation: Verify these are legitimate shared mailboxes"
1.2.2.description: Verify shared mailbox accounts have sign-in disabled using Graph API (app-only auth compatible)
1.2.2.remediation:
# Disable account for a specific user/shared mailbox
Update-MgUser -UserId <UserObjectId> -AccountEnabled:$false

# Disable all disabled users (use with caution)
$DisabledUsers = Get-MgUser -Filter "accountEnabled eq false"
$DisabledUsers | ForEach-Object { Update-MgUser -UserId $_.Id -AccountEnabled:$false }

1.3.1.title: Ensure the 'Password expiration policy' is set to 'Set passwords to never expire'
1.3.1.description: Verify password expiration policy is set to never expire

1.3.2.title: Ensure 'Idle session timeout' is set to '3 hours (or less)' for unmanaged devices
commands: if ($TimeSpan -le $BenchmarkTimeSpan) { Write-Host "** PASS ** Timeout is set to $TimeoutReadable." } else { Write-Host "** FAIL ** Timeout is too long." }
} else { Write-Host "** FAIL **: Idle session timeout is not configured." }
1.3.2.description: Verify idle session timeout is configured for 3 hours or less
1.3.2.remediation: Configure Conditional Access policy to set session timeout to 3 hours or less for unmanaged devices.

1.3.3.title: Ensure 'External sharing' of calendars is not available
1.3.3.description: Verify external calendar sharing is disabled

1.3.4.title: Ensure 'User owned apps and services' is restricted
1.3.4.description: Verify users cannot install add-ins in Office applications

1.3.5.title: Ensure internal phishing protection for Forms is enabled
1.3.5.description: Verify Forms phishing protection is enabled through audit logs

1.3.6.title: Ensure the customer lockbox feature is enabled
1.3.6.description: Verify customer lockbox feature is enabled

1.3.7.title: Ensure 'third-party storage services' are restricted in 'Microsoft 365 on the web'
commands: if ((-not $SP) -or $SP.AccountEnabled) { Write-Host "Audit Result: ** FAIL **" } else { Write-Host "Audit Result: ** PASS **" }
1.3.7.description: Verify third-party storage services are restricted

1.3.9.title: Ensure shared bookings pages are restricted to select users
1.3.9.description: Verify shared bookings pages are restricted to internal users only

2.1.1.title: Ensure Safe Links for Office Applications is Enabled
2.1.1.description: Verify Safe Links protection is enabled for Office applications

2.1.2.title: Ensure the Common Attachment Types Filter is enabled
2.1.2.description: Verify common attachment types filter is enabled

2.1.3.title: Ensure notifications for internal users sending malware is Enabled
2.1.3.description: Verify malware notifications are enabled for internal users

2.1.4.title: Ensure Safe Attachments policy is enabled
2.1.4.description: Verify Safe Attachments policy exists and is enabled
2.1.4.remediation:
Set-SafeAttachmentPolicy -Identity 'Example policy' -Action 'Block' -QuarantineTag 'AdminOnlyAccessPolicy' -Enable $true
OR
New-SafeAttachmentPolicy -Name "CIS 2.1.4" -Enable $true -Action 'Block' -QuarantineTag 'AdminOnlyAccessPolicy'
New-SafeAttachmentRule -Name "CIS 2.1.4 Rule" -SafeAttachmentPolicy "CIS 2.1.4" -RecipientDomainIs 'exampledomain.com'

2.1.5.title: Ensure Safe Attachments for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Microsoft Teams is Enabled
2.1.5.description: Verify ATP Safe Attachments is enabled for SPO/OneDrive/Teams

2.1.6.title: Ensure Exchange Online Spam Policies are set to notify administrators

2.1.7.title: Ensure that an anti-phishing policy has been created
2.1.7.description: Verify anti-phishing policy is configured and enabled

2.1.8.title: Ensure that SPF records are published for all Exchange Domains
2.1.8.description: Verify SPF records exist for all accepted domains
2.1.8.remediation: Create SPF records for each domain: v=spf1 include:spf.protection.outlook.com ~all

2.1.9.title: Ensure that DKIM is enabled for all Exchange Online Domains
commands: [PSCustomObject]@{ DomainName = $Domain.Name; Enabled = [bool]$DKIM.Enabled; Status = if ($DKIM) { $DKIM.Status } else { "Not Configured" }; IsCISCompliant = ($DKIM.Enabled -and $DKIM.Status -eq "Valid") }
2.1.9.description: Verify DKIM is enabled for all domains
2.1.9.remediation: Enable DKIM for each domain using: Enable-DkimSigningConfig -Identity domain.onmicrosoft.com

2.1.10.title: Ensure DMARC records for all Exchange Online domains are published
2.1.10.description: Verify DMARC records are published for all domains
2.1.10.remediation: Create DMARC records for each domain: v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:admin@domain.com; ruf=mailto:admin@domain.com

2.1.11.title: Ensure comprehensive attachment filtering is applied
2.1.11.description: Verify comprehensive attachment filtering is enabled
2.1.11.remediation: Create comprehensive attachment filtering policy blocking dangerous file extensions.

2.1.12.title: Ensure the connection filter IP allow list is not used
2.1.12.description: Verify IP allow list is not in use or is empty

2.1.13.title: Ensure the connection filter safe list is off
2.1.13.description: Verify connection filter safe list is disabled

2.1.14.title: Ensure inbound anti-spam policies do not contain allowed domains
2.1.14.description: Verify no domains are whitelisted in anti-spam policies

2.1.15.title: Ensure outbound anti-spam message limits are in place
2.1.15.description: Verify outbound spam limits are configured
2.1.15.remediation:
$params = @{ RecipientLimitExternalPerHour = 500; RecipientLimitInternalPerHour = 1000; RecipientLimitPerDay = 1000; ActionWhenThresholdReached = 'BlockUser'; NotifyOutboundSpamRecipients = @('admin@example.com','security@example.com') }
Set-HostedOutboundSpamFilterPolicy -Identity 'Default' @params

2.4.1.title: Ensure Priority account protection is enabled and configured
2.4.1.description: Verify priority account protection settings are configured
2.4.1.remediation: Enable priority account protection in Microsoft Defender for Office 365.

2.4.2.title: Ensure Priority accounts have 'Strict protection' presets applied
2.4.2.description: Verify strict conditional access policies are applied to priority accounts
2.4.2.remediation: Apply Strict preset security policies to all priority accounts in Defender for Office 365.

6.1.2.title: Ensure mailbox audit actions are configured
6.1.2.description: Verify mailbox audit actions and retention are configured

6.1.3.title: Ensure 'AuditBypassEnabled' is not enabled on mailboxes
6.1.3.description: Verify audit bypass is not enabled on user mailboxes
6.1.3.remediation:
$MBXAudit = Get-MailboxAuditBypassAssociation -ResultSize unlimited | Where-Object { $_.AuditBypassEnabled -eq $true }
foreach ($mailbox in $MBXAudit) { Set-MailboxAuditBypassAssociation -Identity $mailbox.Name -AuditBypassEnabled $false }

Write-Host "Graph API: Available via beta endpoints under /teams or /teamwork"

Set-CsTeamsClientConfiguration @Params
